content.claim: Geographic settlement patterns independently produce school stratification: pupils from small villages attend single-class primary schools with no internal segregation, but transition to vocational schools at higher rates than urban peers, independent of academic tracking mechanisms
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Illustrative rather than current quantified data
Shows correlation between settlement size and school type, not direct attribution to tracking policy
Does not quantify proportion of total segregation attributable to geographic versus policy-driven factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
